nameMeanings.name: Petersson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Berno, Bernt, Bjorn, Erik, Fredrik, Gudrun, Holger, Joakim, Karsten, Knut, Niklas, Per.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Petter, a vernacular form of Peter . Compare Peterson 2 and Petterson .

variants.name: Petterson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Aksel, Arnt, Bernt, Lennart, Nels, Sig, Swen.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Pettersson  . This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian and Danish cognate   Pettersen  . Compare   Peterson   2.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: Trotte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Cosmo, Rocco, Salvatore, Concetta, Giuseppe, Marco, Tommaso.

variants.name: Trotta
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Vito, Aniello, Attilio, Carlo, Marzio, Nicolina, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore, Amedeo, Carmela.

variants.name: Trotti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luciano, Rocco.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Trotto  .

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

historicEvents.title: The Hatuna Games
historicEvents.description: A conflict on September 29, 1306, between Birger, King of Sweden, and his brothers, the dukes Eric Magnusson and Valdemar Magnusson is known as The Hatuna Games. The brothers arrived to a party late in the night at King Birger's estate. The dukes captured and imprisoned Birger and his wife in Nykoping Castle's dungeon. Duke Eric then gained power over Sweden.
Black Death killed one-third of population.

en.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson was born as the son of Peter and Unknown. He had at least 4 sons and 6 daughters with Ingegard Johansdotter. He died in 1364, in Eka, Uppsala, Sweden.

fr.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson est né, fils de Peter et Unknown. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles avec Ingegard Johansdotter. Il est décédé en 1364, à Eka, Uppsala, Suède.

de.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson wurde, als Sohn von Peter und Unknown geboren. Er hatte mit Ingegard Johansdotter mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb 1364 in Eka, Uppsala, Schweden.

it.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson è nato il come figlio di Peter e Unknown. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie con Ingegard Johansdotter. Morì nel 1364, nel Eka, Uppsala, Svezia.

ja.lifeSummary: Trotte PeterssonはPeterとUnknownの息子として生まれた。彼はIngegard Johansdotterとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼は1364年にスウェーデンウップサラエカで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson(은)는 Peter(와)과 Unknown의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Ingegard Johansdotter(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1364년 스웨덴 업프사라 카에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson nasceu filho de Peter e Unknown. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas com Ingegard Johansdotter. Ele faleceu em 1364, em Eka, Uppsala, Suécia.

ru.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson родился. Его родителей звали Peter и Unknown. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей от Ingegard Johansdotter. Он умер в 1364 г. в местности эка, уппсала, Швеция.

es.lifeSummary: Trotte Petersson nació como hijo de Peter y Unknown. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas con Ingegard Johansdotter. Murió en 1364, en Eka, Uppsala, Suecia.
